framework.reasoning: Paper uses POT (Python Optimal Transport) for LP-Wasserstein, SciPy for graph ops, and a transformer-based forecasting head. PyTorch is standard for the forecasting head. The core geometric pipeline is pure NumPy/SciPy/POT and is CPU-sufficient. CUDA optional for Kronos head training.

classes.name: AgentGraph
classes.description: Maintains rolling windowed action-agreement graph. Sparsifies at w0=0.5.

classes.name: OllivierRicciComputer
methods: compute(G: scipy.sparse.csr_matrix, alpha: float=0.5) -> Dict[Tuple[int,int], float]
classes.description: Computes per-edge ORC via LP W1 (POT). Returns kappa dict keyed by (i,j). Sign split at ±0.1.

classes.name: DiscreteRicciFlow
methods: run(G: scipy.sparse.csr_matrix, kappa_dict: Dict, step_size: float=0.01, max_iter: int=1000) -> Tuple[float, List[float]]
classes.description: Runs discrete Ricci flow from snapshot G_t. Returns tau_sing = first neckpinch time. Update rule: w(e) <- w(e) * (1 - step_size * kappa(e)). ASSUMED: multiplicative update; see risk R1.

classes.name: FSQVocabularyTracker
classes.description: Fixed 3D FSQ codebook (Ld=4, K=64). Projects agent action vectors to nearest codeword index. V_eff = exp(H(p_t)) where H is entropy of codebook utilization counts.

classes.name: CUSUMDetector
methods: set_baseline(baseline_mean: float, k: float, h: float) -> None
classes.description: One-sided Page CUSUM (1954). Maintains running S_t. Returns (S_t, alarm_fired). Baseline estimated from first W=35 observations.

classes.name: KendallTauDetector
classes.description: Rolling Kendall-tau trend test over window W_tau. Fires alarm if tau > tau_thresh=-0.4.

classes.name: ContagionDetector
classes.description: OR-combination of CUSUMDetector and KendallTauDetector on beta_minus series.

classes.name: GeomHerdPipeline
classes.description: Top-level pipeline. Calls graph construction -> ORC -> flow -> V_eff -> detectors. Returns GeomHerdOutput dataclass containing all scalars and alarm booleans.

classes.name: CWSSubstrate
classes.description: Cividino-Sornette continuous-spin model. N=66 agents, na=4 assets. Coupling kappa controls herding strength. Each agent's action drawn from P(a_i | M_t, kappa, noise). Linear price impact.

classes.name: PersonaAgent
methods: set_persona(risk_appetite: float, momentum_horizon: int, herding_tendency: float) -> None
classes.description: Wraps an LLM call (default: Claude via Anthropic API or stub rule-based fallback). Returns action in {buy, hold, sell}. ASSUMED: persona prompt template; actual prompts withheld by authors.

classes.name: RuleBasedAgentFallback
classes.description: Deterministic fallback (no LLM): noise trader / fundamental / momentum archetypes. Used when LLM_MODE=false in config.

files.sir_mapping: Vicsek particle model for OOD transfer

classes.name: VicsekSubstrate
classes.description: N=600 particles, constant speed, heading alignment under angular noise eta. kNN graph (k=10) on heading sequence for agent graph construction.

classes.name: PriceTokeniser
classes.description: Learned VQ-VAE tokeniser on OHLCV sequences. Frozen during Kronos head training. ASSUMED architecture: 2-layer MLP encoder + codebook of size 512.

classes.name: AdaLNZero
classes.description: Adaptive layer-norm with zero-init gating, conditioned on GeomHerd triplet. Modulates transformer hidden states.

classes.name: KronosHead
classes.description: Small transformer (ASSUMED: 2 layers, 4 heads, d_model=64) conditioned on GeomHerd triplet via AdaLN-Zero. Only conditioning layers trained; price tokeniser frozen.

classes.name: DetectionMetrics
methods: precision_recall_far(alarms: List, events: List, subcritical_mask: List) -> dict
paired_bootstrap_lead(lead_A: List, lead_B: List, n_boot: int=5000) -> dict
classes.description: All detection metrics from Table 2/3: precision, recall_super, FAR_sub, AUROC, AUPRC, conditional median lead time with 95% CI, paired bootstrap difference.

classes.name: ForecastMetrics
classes.description: Interquartile-mean MAE (rliable library) for cascade-window forecasting comparison.

classes.name: LSVBaseline
classes.description: Lakonishok-Shleifer-Vishny (1992) institutional buy/sell imbalance statistic.

classes.name: CSADBaseline
methods: cck_regression(csad: np.ndarray, rm: np.ndarray, kappa_or: np.ndarray) -> dict
classes.description: CCK (2000) cross-sectional absolute deviation. Also implements augmented regression (Eq. 8) with HAC Newey-West SEs.

classes.name: GeomHerdConfig
classes.description: Dataclass holding all hyperparameters. Loaded from config.yaml.

tensor_flow.name: GeomHerd core forward pass (per snapshot)
pseudocode: INPUT: actions[N, Tw] int — rolling window of agent actions
STEP 1 (AgentGraph): w[N,N] = windowed_agreement(actions, Tw=100); sparsify at w0=0.5 → G_t sparse
STEP 2 (LazyWalk): for each edge (i,j): mu_i[N], mu_j[N] = alpha*delta + (1-alpha)*w_row_normalized
STEP 3 (ORC via LP-W1): kappa[(i,j)] = 1 - W1(mu_i, mu_j) / w[i,j]  for each edge; uses POT ot.emd2
STEP 4 (Sign decomp): E_plus = {e: kappa[e]>0.1}; E_minus = {e: kappa[e]<-0.1}
STEP 5 (Scalars): kappa_bar_plus = mean(kappa[e] for e in E_plus); beta_minus = |E_minus|/|E|
STEP 6 (Ricci flow): fresh flow from G_t → tau_sing scalar (first neckpinch step)
OUTPUT: GeomHerdOutput(kappa_bar_plus: float, beta_minus: float, tau_sing: float, V_eff: float, alarm_plus: bool, alarm_minus: bool)

tensor_flow.name: Kronos forecasting head forward pass
pseudocode: INPUT: ohlcv[B, T_ctx, 5] float32 — OHLCV context window
STEP 1 (PriceTokeniser, frozen): tokens[B, T_ctx] int = vq_encode(ohlcv)
STEP 2 (Embedding): h[B, T_ctx, d_model] = embed(tokens) + pos_enc
STEP 3 (AdaLN-Zero): scale, shift = linear(triplet) → [B, 2*d_model]; zero-init weights
STEP 4 (Transformer layers): for each layer: h = adaLN_zero(h, scale, shift); h = attn(h); h = ffn(h)

config_schema.content_description: See architecture_plan_summary.md for full YAML template

rationale.POT: LP Wasserstein-1 via ot.emd2 — explicitly required (Appendix C)
rationale.scipy: CSR graph, Dijkstra all-pairs paths, Kendall-tau (scipy.stats)
rationale.statsmodels: HAC Newey-West standard errors for CCK regression
rationale.anthropic: LLM agent calls (Claude API); can be mocked with rule-based fallback

entrypoints.description: Run GeomHerd detection pipeline on CWS or Vicsek substrate
Run all baseline detectors (LSV, CSAD, Sandhu, Srinivasan, Lap-CSAD, Jiang, AA-MI)
Compute all metrics (Table 2, Table 3) from saved detector outputs
Train Kronos forecasting head on CWS trajectories
Augmented CCK regression (Eq. 8) across supercritical seeds

docker_spec.note: No GPU base needed for geometric pipeline; use pytorch/pytorch:2.1.0-cuda11.8-... base if training Kronos head

risk_assessment.description: Discrete Ricci flow update rule not specified in paper (confidence 0.60). Neckpinch stopping criterion given but not per-step update.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement DiscreteRicciFlow as abstract base class with swappable update strategy. Default to multiplicative: w <- w*(1 - lr*kappa). Add --flow_variant config flag.

risk_assessment.description: Kronos head architecture (layers, hidden dim, heads) absent from paper (confidence 0.45). Any implementation is approximate.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use small configurable transformer (n_layers, n_heads, d_model all in config.yaml with ASSUMED comments). Accept that forecasting MAE numbers may not reproduce exactly.

risk_assessment.description: LLM persona prompts intentionally withheld (confidence 0.55). Geometric pipeline is LLM-agnostic, but richer action diversity requires good prompts.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement RuleBasedAgentFallback as default. Provide example persona prompt template in configs/persona_template.yaml. Allow --llm_mode flag to swap in real LLM.

risk_assessment.description: Kendall-tau threshold tau_thresh and W_tau not given numerically (confidence 0.65).
risk_assessment.mitigation: Default tau_thresh=-0.4 (inferred from Table 3). Expose as config param. Include calibration sweep script.

risk_assessment.description: CWS substrate mechanics described at high level; full parameterization requires Cividino et al. 2023 paper.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement CWS from paper description + supplementary. Add reference comment pointing to original paper. Validate that simulated returns reproduce stylized facts (tail index, vol-ACF) cited in paper.

risk_assessment.description: POT LP Wasserstein is O(N^3 log N) per edge pair. For N=66 agents with ~200 edges per snapshot, this may be slow at scale.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use POT's sparse/batch API. Add optional --fast_ot flag for Sinkhorn approximation (though ablation shows Sinkhorn fails; LP is required for correctness).
